examples: The picture actually shows a child's neocortex‚ÄĒa part of the brain that controls sight and hearing.
The power of the neocortex comes from its ability to think beyond the present, concrete moment.
The neocortex is also responsible for the process by which we decide on and commit to particular courses of action.
Hawkins believes that the several levels of the neocortex are an organizational hierarchy of sensory inputs.
His thesis for how the neocortex works is that it has a uniform algorithm.
